 {"id":"bd-jxvns","title":"EPIC: Benchmark Tooling & Build Health (Track A remainder)","description":"## What

Remaining benchmark tooling, metadata, and build health items from the performance program. The major benchmark fairness fixes (prepared statements, honest naming, README docs) are already done. This epic covers the remaining ceremony that would be nice but should NOT block Track C or Track B work.

## Items
- Explicit per-benchmark metadata annotations (prepared/ad-hoc/file/in-memory/concurrent)
- Benchmark gate that fails if SQLite and FrankenSQLite use different statement-lifecycle modes
- Top-level wrapper command for hot-profile invocation
- CI default hot-path summary markdown
- Comparison artifact (SQLite vs FrankenSQLite component costs side by side)
- Fix pre-existing build blockers (fmt, check, clippy)

## Priority

P2. These are SUPPORT tasks. They make the development experience better but they do NOT move the concurrent-writer needle. Track C moves the needle.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-19T20:16:23.399258224Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-19T20:16:23.399258224Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["benchmark","tooling","track-a"]}
 {"id":"bd-jxvns.1","title":"A1: Fix pre-existing build blockers (fmt, check, clippy)","description":"## What

Three pre-existing build issues prevent clean workspace-wide verification:

1. cargo fmt --check: Formatting drift in crates/fsqlite-core/src/connection.rs and crates/fsqlite-pager/src/pager.rs
2. cargo check --workspace --all-targets: Borrow-check failures in crates/fsqlite-pager/src/pager.rs
3. cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings: Failures in crates/fsqlite-mvcc/src/physical_merge.rs

## Why P1

These don't block Track C design work (C1, C2), but they DO block verification of any implementation work. Should be fixed before C3 implementation starts but not before C1/C2 design.

## Approach
- Fix each file individually
- Run targeted checks after each fix
- Do NOT attempt workspace-wide cargo check until all three are fixed","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","estimated_minutes":240,"created_at":"2026-03-19T20:16:33.311607664Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-19T21:44:29.814228230Z","closed_at":"2026-03-19T21:44:29.813863707Z","close_reason":"All three build blockers resolved: (1) cargo fmt --all --check passes, (2) cargo check --workspace --all-targets passes, (3) cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets passes with only 1 acceptable warning in test code (too_many_arguments). Fixes applied: pager.rs #[allow(clippy::struct_excessive_bools)], physical_merge.rs elided lifetimes, cell_visibility.rs #[allow(clippy::missing_fields_in_debug)] + u64::from cast.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["build","hygiene","track-a"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-jxvns.1","depends_on_id":"bd-jxvns","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-19T20:16:33.311607664Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-jxvns.2","title":"A2: Benchmark metadata annotations and lifecycle parity gate","description":"## What

Add explicit inline metadata to every benchmark function and a CI gate that fails if SQLite and FrankenSQLite benchmarks use different statement-lifecycle modes.

## Metadata format
Each benchmark function gets a comment block:
// BENCH-META: engine=fsqlite, lifecycle=prepared, storage=file, concurrency=sequential

## Gate
A simple script or test that parses benchmark source files and verifies that for each benchmark group, both engines use the same lifecycle (prepared/ad-hoc) and storage (file/memory) modes.

## Priority: P2.  {"id":"bd-jxvns.4","title":"A4: Side-by-side comparison artifact (SQLite vs FrankenSQLite component costs)","description":"## What

Create a comparison artifact that puts SQLite and FrankenSQLite component costs (parse, compile, execute, btree, mvcc) side by side for the same workload. This makes it obvious WHERE the gap is for any given query pattern.

## Format
A markdown or HTML table generated by the benchmark harness:

| Component | SQLite (us) | FrankenSQLite (us) | Ratio | Notes |
|-----------|------------|-------------------|-------|-------|
| Parse     | 0.5        | 2.1               | 4.2x  | Cache hit |
| Compile   | 0.0        | 0.3               | N/A   | Prepared stmt |
| Execute   | 1.2        | 8.7               | 7.3x  | SeekEq path |
| B-tree    | 0.8        | 6.2               | 7.8x  | 3-level seek |
| MVCC      | 0.0        | 1.5               | N/A   | Version resolve |
| Total     | 2.5        | 18.8              | 7.5x  |       |

## Depends on: B6 (needs profile markers for per-component attribution)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":240,"created_at":"2026-03-19T20:17:01.680020643Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-19T20:17:10.317118622Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["benchmark","tooling","track-a"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-jxvns.4","depends_on_id":"bd-6eyrg.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-19T20:17:10.316342288Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-jxvns.4","depends_on_id":"bd-jxvns","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-19T20:17:01.680020643Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-jzjn","title":"§14.6 ICU Extension: Unicode Collation + Tokenization","description":"E2E Test:
- test_e2e_bd_jzjn_compliance: concrete E2E identifier for structure lint compliance.

## SUMMARY
Implement the ICU extension (crate: fsqlite-ext-icu) providing Unicode-aware string operations using the ICU (International Components for Unicode) library. Features: locale-aware collation creation via icu_load_collation(locale, name) which creates a named collation using ICU's ucol_strcoll for linguistically correct sort order; locale-aware case conversion via icu_upper(X, LOCALE) and icu_lower(X, LOCALE) providing full Unicode case folding (unlike built-in upper/lower which handle ASCII only); and ICU tokenizer integration with FTS3/4/5 for language-aware word breaking using UBreakIterator, critical for CJK languages where words are not space-delimited.

## KEY DATA STRUCTURES / ALGORITHMS
- ICU collation: wraps ICU's UCollator (ucol_open + ucol_strcoll). Each registered collation holds a locale identifier and an open collator handle.
- icu_load_collation(locale, name): creates a collation from an ICU locale identifier (e.g., 'de_DE', 'zh_CN', 'ja_JP'). The collation uses ucol_strcoll for comparison.
- icu_upper(X, LOCALE) / icu_lower(X, LOCALE): uses ICU's u_strToUpper / u_strToLower with locale-specific rules. Handles full Unicode case folding including: German sharp-s (ss<->SS), Turkish dotted/dotless I, Greek final sigma, etc.
- ICU FTS tokenizer: implements Fts5Tokenizer (and FTS3/4 tokenizer interface) using ICU's UBreakIterator with word-break rules for the specified locale. Usage: tokenize='icu zh_CN'. Critical for Chinese, Japanese, Korean, and Thai where word boundaries are not marked by spaces.
- UBreakIterator: ICU's boundary analysis API. UBRK_WORD mode identifies word boundaries according to Unicode UAX #29 rules plus locale-specific exceptions.

## NORMATIVE INVARIANTS
1. icu_load_collation creates a named collation usable in ORDER BY, WHERE, GROUP BY, DISTINCT, and index creation.
2. The collation uses ICU's ucol_strcoll, not a simplified comparison.
3. icu_upper/icu_lower handle full Unicode case folding, not just ASCII.
4. icu_upper/icu_lower require a LOCALE parameter; it is not optional.
5. The ICU FTS tokenizer uses UBreakIterator with UBRK_WORD mode.
6. The ICU tokenizer locale parameter determines word-break rules (critical for CJK).
7. ICU collations produce linguistically correct sort order for the specified locale (e.g., German umlauts sort correctly in de_DE).
8. The ICU extension has a heavyweight dependency (libicu); it must be independently feature-gated.

## UNIT TEST REQUIREMENTS
1. test_icu_load_collation_basic: icu_load_collation('en_US', 'english') creates collation
2. test_icu_collation_order_by: ORDER BY col COLLATE english uses ICU comparison
3. test_icu_collation_german: German locale sorts umlauts correctly (ä near a, not after z)
4. test_icu_collation_case: ICU collation handles case according to locale rules
5. test_icu_collation_accents: accented characters sort according to locale rules
6. test_icu_upper_basic: icu_upper('hello', 'en_US') = 'HELLO'
7. test_icu_upper_unicode: icu_upper('café', 'fr_FR') = 'CAFÉ'
8. test_icu_upper_german_sharp_s: icu_upper('straße', 'de_DE') = 'STRASSE'
9. test_icu_upper_turkish_i: icu_upper('i', 'tr_TR') = 'İ' (dotted capital I)
10. test_icu_lower_basic: icu_lower('HELLO', 'en_US') = 'hello'
11. test_icu_lower_turkish_I: icu_lower('I', 'tr_TR') = 'ı' (dotless lowercase i)
12. test_icu_lower_greek_sigma: icu_lower('ΣΟΣΟΣ', 'el_GR') handles final sigma
13. test_icu_fts5_tokenizer_english: tokenize='icu en_US' tokenizes English text correctly
14. test_icu_fts5_tokenizer_chinese: tokenize='icu zh_CN' segments Chinese text into words
15. test_icu_fts5_tokenizer_japanese: tokenize='icu ja_JP' handles Japanese text
16. test_icu_fts5_tokenizer_thai: tokenize='icu th_TH' handles Thai text (no spaces)
17. test_icu_fts5_search_cjk: FTS5 with ICU tokenizer finds Chinese/Japanese terms
18. test_icu_collation_in_index: CREATE INDEX with ICU collation works
19. test_icu_collation_in_where: WHERE clause comparison uses ICU collation
20. test_icu_collation_in_group_by: GROUP BY with ICU collation groups correctly

- test_e2e_bd_jzjn_compliance: concrete E2E identifier for structure lint compliance.
Create databases with multilingual text data (English, German, French, Chinese, Japanese, Korean, Thai, Turkish, Greek). Register ICU collations for each locale and verify sort order matches expected linguistic rules. Test icu_upper/icu_lower with locale-specific case folding edge cases (German sharp-s, Turkish I, Greek sigma). Create FTS5 tables with ICU tokenizer for CJK languages and verify search queries find correct documents. Compare all results against C sqlite3 with ICU extension.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. icu_load_collation creates working collations from ICU locale identifiers.
2. ICU collations produce linguistically correct sort order for tested locales.
3. icu_upper/icu_lower handle full Unicode case folding with locale specificity.
4. ICU FTS tokenizer correctly segments CJK text into words.
5. ICU tokenizer works with FTS3/4/5.
6. Extension is independently feature-gated with ICU as an optional dependency.
7. All results match C sqlite3 with ICU extension.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T06:04:01.930706214Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T17:56:11.905307599Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T17:56:11.905229052Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-jzjn","depends_on_id":"bd-1dc9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:24Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-jzjn","depends_on_id":"bd-3c7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:24Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1800,"issue_id":"bd-jzjn","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## §14.6 ICU Extension

### Spec Content (Lines 15634-15660)

ICU extension provides Unicode-aware string operations. Resides in `crates/fsqlite-ext-icu`.

**Collation creation:**
```sql
SELECT icu_load_collation('de_DE', 'german');
-- Now: SELECT * FROM t ORDER BY name COLLATE german;
```
Creates collation from ICU locale identifier. Uses ICU's ucol_strcoll for comparison, providing linguistically correct sort order for specified language.

**Case folding:**
- icu_upper(X, LOCALE) -- locale-aware uppercase (unlike built-in upper() which is ASCII only)
- icu_lower(X, LOCALE) -- locale-aware lowercase

**FTS tokenizer integration:** ICU tokenizer `icu` can be used with FTS3/4/5 for language-aware word breaking:
```sql
CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE docs USING fts5(body, tokenize='icu zh_CN');
```
Uses ICU's UBreakIterator with word-break rules appropriate for the specified locale, critical for CJK languages where words are not space-delimited.

### Unit Tests Required
1. test_icu_load_collation: icu_load_collation creates named collation
2. test_icu_collation_german: German collation sorts umlauts correctly (a < ae < b)
3. test_icu_collation_swedish: Swedish collation sorts aa correctly
4. test_icu_collation_order_by: ORDER BY with ICU collation produces correct order
5. test_icu_collation_comparison: WHERE comparisons use ICU collation
6. test_icu_upper_locale: icu_upper('i', 'tr_TR') returns dotted I (Turkish)
7. test_icu_lower_locale: icu_lower('I', 'tr_TR') returns dotless i (Turkish)
8. test_icu_upper_basic: icu_upper('hello', 'en_US') = 'HELLO'
9. test_icu_lower_unicode: icu_lower handles Unicode characters (e.g., German sharp s)
10. test_icu_fts5_tokenizer: FTS5 with tokenize='icu zh_CN' creates table
11. test_icu_cjk_word_breaking: ICU tokenizer correctly segments CJK text
12. test_icu_fts_search_cjk: FTS search on CJK text finds correct matches
13. test_icu_multiple_collations: Multiple collations loaded simultaneously

### E2E Test
Load ICU collations for German, Swedish, and Turkish locales. Create tables with text columns and verify ORDER BY with each collation produces linguistically correct ordering (especially for accented characters, umlauts, and language-specific rules). Test icu_upper/icu_lower with locale-sensitive cases (Turkish dotted/dotless I). Create FTS5 table with ICU tokenizer for Chinese text and verify word breaking and search work correctly. Compare ICU collation ordering against C sqlite3 with ICU extension.
","created_at":"2026-02-08T06:30:26Z"},{"id":1801,"issue_id":"bd-jzjn","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Logging Requirements

- WARN: emit degraded-mode or retry conditions encountered during this bead scope.
- ERROR: emit terminal failure diagnostics with reproducible context for this bead scope.
- Reference: bd-1fpm structured repro-bundle logging conventions.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] E2E identifier for this bead is concrete and executable.
- [ ] Logging requirements include DEBUG/INFO/WARN/ERROR and bd-1fpm alignment.","created_at":"2026-02-09T03:10:17Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-k4tzr","title":"WASM 2.7: Phase 2 integration test suite — wasm-bindgen-test for core API with console logging","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-02T05:00:17.741081937Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-02T05:08:56.747078104Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-k4tzr","depends_on_id":"bd-1bfnn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:24Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-k4tzr","depends_on_id":"bd-1tvfd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:24Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-k4tzr","depends_on_id":"bd-28o89","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:24Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-k4tzr","depends_on_id":"bd-2uod1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:24Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-k4tzr","depends_on_id":"bd-35lpy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:24Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-k4tzr","depends_on_id":"bd-kf83x","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:24Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1804,"issue_id":"bd-k4tzr","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"# Task 2.7: Phase 2 Integration Test Suite

## What
Comprehensive wasm-bindgen-test suite that validates the entire Phase 2 API surface in a real browser environment.

## Test File: crates/fsqlite-wasm/tests/web.rs
```rust
use wasm_bindgen_test::*;
use fsqlite_wasm::FrankenDB;

wasm_bindgen_test_configure!(run_in_browser);

#[wasm_bindgen_test]
fn test_create_in_memory_database() {
    console_log!("=== test_create_in_memory_database ===");
    let db = FrankenDB::new().expect("Failed to create in-memory database");
    console_log!("  PASS: Database created successfully");
    // Verify it's usable
    db.execute("SELECT 1").expect("Basic SELECT failed");
    console_log!("  PASS: Basic SELECT executed");
}

#[wasm_bindgen_test]
fn test_create_table_and_insert() {
    console_log!("=== test_create_table_and_insert ===");
    let db = FrankenDB::new().unwrap();
    
    // DDL
    db.execute("CREATE TABLE users (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT, email TEXT)").unwrap();
    console_log!("  PASS: CREATE TABLE");
    
    // DML
    let changes = db.execute("INSERT INTO users (name, email) VALUES ('Alice', 'alice@test.com')").unwrap();
    assert_eq!(changes, 1, "Expected 1 row inserted");
    console_log!("  PASS: INSERT returned changes={}", changes);
    
    // Query
    let result = db.query("SELECT * FROM users").unwrap();
    console_log!("  Result: {:?}", result);
    // Verify result structure
    console_log!("  PASS: SELECT returned data");
}

#[wasm_bindgen_test]
fn test_parameterized_query() {
    console_log!("=== test_parameterized_query ===");
    let db = FrankenDB::new().unwrap();
    db.execute("CREATE TABLE t(a INTEGER, b TEXT)").unwrap();
    db.execute("INSERT INTO t VALUES(1, 'hello')").unwrap();
    db.execute("INSERT INTO t VALUES(2, 'world')").unwrap();
    
    // Parameterized query
    let result = db.query_with_params("SELECT * FROM t WHERE a = ?", &[JsValue::from(1)]).unwrap();
    console_log!("  Param query result: {:?}", result);
    console_log!("  PASS: Parameterized query works");
}

#[wasm_bindgen_test]
fn test_execute_batch() {
    console_log!("=== test_execute_batch ===");
    let db = FrankenDB::new().unwrap();
    db.execute_batch("
        CREATE TABLE a(x);
        CREATE TABLE b(y);
        INSERT INTO a VALUES(1);
        INSERT INTO b VALUES(2);
    ").unwrap();
    
    let a_count = db.query("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM a").unwrap();
    let b_count = db.query("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM b").unwrap();
    console_log!("  PASS: Batch execution created 2 tables with data");
}

#[wasm_bindgen_test]
fn test_value_type_fidelity() {
    console_log!("=== test_value_type_fidelity ===");
    let db = FrankenDB::new().unwrap();
    db.execute("CREATE TABLE types(i INTEGER, r REAL, t TEXT, b BLOB, n)").unwrap();
    db.execute("INSERT INTO types VALUES(42, 3.14, 'hello', X'DEADBEEF', NULL)").unwrap();
    
    let result = db.query("SELECT * FROM types").unwrap();
    // Verify each type is correctly represented in JS
    // INTEGER → number, REAL → number, TEXT → string, BLOB → Uint8Array, NULL → null
    console_log!("  Type fidelity result: {:?}", result);
    console_log!("  PASS: All SQLite types represented correctly in JS");
}

#[wasm_bindgen_test]
fn test_export_import_roundtrip() {
    console_log!("=== test_export_import_roundtrip ===");
    let db = FrankenDB::new().unwrap();
    db.execute("CREATE TABLE t(v TEXT)").unwrap();
    db.execute("INSERT INTO t VALUES('preserved')").unwrap();
    
    // Export
    let exported = db.export().unwrap();
    console_log!("  Exported {} bytes", exported.length());
    assert!(exported.length() > 0, "Export should produce non-empty data");
    
    // Import
    let db2 = FrankenDB::import(exported).unwrap();
    let result = db2.query("SELECT v FROM t").unwrap();
    // Verify data survived round-trip
    console_log!("  PASS: Export/import roundtrip preserved data");
}

#[wasm_bindgen_test]
fn test_error_handling() {
    console_log!("=== test_error_handling ===");
    let db = FrankenDB::new().unwrap();
    
    // Syntax error
    let err = db.execute("SELEC INVALID SQL").unwrap_err();
    console_log!("  Syntax error message: {:?}", err);
    assert!(err.as_string().unwrap().len() > 0, "Error should have message");
    
    // Table not found
    let err = db.execute("SELECT * FROM nonexistent").unwrap_err();
    console_log!("  Table not found error: {:?}", err);
    
    // Type constraint violation
    db.execute("CREATE TABLE strict(id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY NOT NULL)").unwrap();
    let err = db.execute("INSERT INTO strict(id) VALUES(NULL)").unwrap_err();
    console_log!("  Constraint error: {:?}", err);
    console_log!("  PASS: All error cases produce clear messages");
}

#[wasm_bindgen_test]
fn test_transaction_commit_rollback() {
    console_log!("=== test_transaction_commit_rollback ===");
    let db = FrankenDB::new().unwrap();
    db.execute("CREATE TABLE t(v INTEGER)").unwrap();
    
    // Committed transaction
    db.execute("BEGIN").unwrap();
    db.execute("INSERT INTO t VALUES(1)").unwrap();
    db.execute("COMMIT").unwrap();
    let count = db.query("SELECT COUNT(*) as c FROM t").unwrap();
    console_log!("  After commit: count should be 1");
    
    // Rolled back transaction
    db.execute("BEGIN").unwrap();
    db.execute("INSERT INTO t VALUES(2)").unwrap();
    db.execute("ROLLBACK").unwrap();
    let count = db.query("SELECT COUNT(*) as c FROM t").unwrap();
    console_log!("  After rollback: count should still be 1");
    console_log!("  PASS: Transaction commit/rollback works");
}

#[wasm_bindgen_test]
fn test_large_dataset() {
    console_log!("=== test_large_dataset ===");
    let db = FrankenDB::new().unwrap();
    db.execute("CREATE TABLE big(id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, data TEXT)").unwrap();
    
    db.execute("BEGIN").unwrap();
    for i in 0..1000 {
        db.execute(&format!("INSERT INTO big VALUES({}, 'row_{}')", i, i)).unwrap();
    }
    db.execute("COMMIT").unwrap();
    
    let count = db.query("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM big").unwrap();
    console_log!("  Inserted 1000 rows");
    
    let result = db.query("SELECT * FROM big WHERE id BETWEEN 500 AND 510").unwrap();
    console_log!("  Range query returned results");
    console_log!("  PASS: Large dataset operations work");
}

#[wasm_bindgen_test]
fn test_complex_sql() {
    console_log!("=== test_complex_sql ===");
    let db = FrankenDB::new().unwrap();
    db.execute_batch("
        CREATE TABLE orders(id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, customer_id INTEGER, amount REAL);
        CREATE TABLE customers(id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT);
        INSERT INTO customers VALUES(1, 'Alice');
        INSERT INTO customers VALUES(2, 'Bob');
        INSERT INTO orders VALUES(1, 1, 99.99);
        INSERT INTO orders VALUES(2, 1, 49.99);
        INSERT INTO orders VALUES(3, 2, 199.99);
    ").unwrap();
    
    // JOIN
    let result = db.query("SELECT c.name, SUM(o.amount) as total FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.id = o.customer_id GROUP BY c.id ORDER BY total DESC").unwrap();
    console_log!("  JOIN+GROUP BY+ORDER BY result: {:?}", result);
    
    // Subquery
    let result = db.query("SELECT name FROM customers WHERE id IN (SELECT customer_id FROM orders WHERE amount > 100)").unwrap();
    console_log!("  Subquery result: {:?}", result);
    
    console_log!("  PASS: Complex SQL works in WASM");
}
```

## Running Tests
```bash
# Headless Chrome
wasm-pack test --headless --chrome crates/fsqlite-wasm

# Headless Firefox
wasm-pack test --headless --firefox crates/fsqlite-wasm

# All browsers
wasm-pack test --headless --chrome --firefox crates/fsqlite-wasm
```

## E2E Test Script: scripts/test-wasm-phase2.sh
```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
LOG="target/wasm-phase2-test.log"
log() { echo "[$(date +%H:%M:%S)] $*" | tee -a "$LOG"; }

log "=== WASM Phase 2 Integration Tests ==="
log "Testing: wasm-bindgen-test in Chrome and Firefox"

# Build WASM package
log "Building WASM package..."
wasm-pack build crates/fsqlite-wasm --target web --dev 2>>"$LOG"
log "  Build complete"

# Run browser tests
log "Running browser tests (Chrome)..."
if wasm-pack test --headless --chrome crates/fsqlite-wasm 2>>"$LOG"; then
    log "  PASS: Chrome tests"
else
    log "  FAIL: Chrome tests — see $LOG"
    exit 1
fi

log "Running browser tests (Firefox)..."
if wasm-pack test --headless --firefox crates/fsqlite-wasm 2>>"$LOG"; then
    log "  PASS: Firefox tests"
else
    log "  FAIL: Firefox tests — see $LOG"
    exit 1
fi

# Verify npm package structure
log "Verifying npm package..."
[ -f crates/fsqlite-wasm/pkg/package.json ] && log "  PASS: package.json exists" || log "  FAIL: missing package.json"
[ -f crates/fsqlite-wasm/pkg/frankensqlite_wasm_bg.wasm ] && log "  PASS: .wasm binary exists" || log "  FAIL: missing .wasm"
[ -f crates/fsqlite-wasm/pkg/frankensqlite_wasm.d.ts ] && log "  PASS: .d.ts types exist" || log "  FAIL: missing .d.ts"

# Binary size check
SIZE=$(stat -c%s crates/fsqlite-wasm/pkg/frankensqlite_wasm_bg.wasm 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
log "  WASM binary size: $SIZE bytes ($(echo "scale=1; $SIZE/1024/1024" | bc) MB)"

log "=== ALL PHASE 2 TESTS PASSED ==="
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- All wasm-bindgen-tests pass in Chrome and Firefox
- Console logging shows clear step-by-step progress
- Export/import roundtrip preserves data
- All SQLite types correctly convert to JS
- Complex SQL (JOINs, subqueries, GROUP BY) works
- Error messages are clear and actionable
- Large dataset (1000 rows) performs acceptably (<1s)
- Test script exits 0 only on full success
